pages.body:
How can one atone for a sin he never committed?
I am just like anyone else, just a bit different,
just like how someone else would be.

pages.title: For Me
pages.body:
A positive outlook you crave,
A little bit of grace, can you save?
Tired all along,
Why the poker face?
A laugh so fake,
With a body and soul so brave
I wonder if I will ever be saved.

A misery in my mind,
A hope with a heart closed in shrine.
Days ago, you were still you…
Ever wonder why the true ones are so few?
I wonder, did you ever grow?

Wait, wait,
and the pain might fade away.
Along the way,
maybe someone will stop,
and you won't fall from the weak rope.

pages.title: For God
pages.body:
Surrounded by high tides,
You wonder if I survive.
My disbelief shook you
For such a task, how much cruelty it took you.

Earn my respect,
I shall ask.
A life must not be wasted in vain.
I don't ask the impossible,
Yet I feel exiled,
Living in disdain.

Answer, my body shivers.
I've been sitting in the rain,
Still holding hope
While walking blindly on a fragile rope.

Try me as much as you want;
I have the guts that others don't.
The wait is never over
But I'll endure, even slower.

pages.title: For You
pages.body:
A random instance you came,
a bliss for a man living in shame.
Connecting so long,
at first it felt wrong.
But as we grew, it turned real, and true.
I fell — you held yourself strong.
Almost like a sad song,
lyrics sharp as heartbreak.
I stopped, for my own sake.
You were still you, still there.
And I fell again,
with no conscience left to think of my gain.

You fell for someone else,
and I was restless.
But when my fuel was gone, and I stopped
you, a shimmer of hope, came back.
I tried to stop myself from falling again.
Now I'm in a pit of flowers,
and there is only you.
Scared to my core,
I fell for your lore.
Mercy, I beg, please don't go.
Stay for life, and we shall grow.

A life so pretty, with no time for despair.
A modern melody of love,
with everything aside, and you above.

pages.title: Warzone
pages.body:
Unarmed in a warzone
I stand with my head down,
Surrounded by hounds,
Cursed by the wind's sound.

Before the sun slips,
I beg for one more dream,
Before I drown in the blue
One last glimpse of you.

Unable to name what is reality,
The dream grows sacred in its fragility.
And it breaks just as I knew it would.

Now I stand under a merciless gaze,
Familiarity running through my veins,
February settling in my bones again.

I fall this time,
Wish I forget to write
To leave it behind in lines,
To survive in rhyme.

pages.title: Wrong
pages.body:
Right into the wrong,
I stepped outside my identity,
where do I belong?

I kept my thoughts on paper,
then burned them in rage.
Now what is left to write,
when all I see is dark with blinded sight?

Out of place, I hide,
breathing without pride.
The blue you see in hindsight
pulls me under every night.

I jumped into a pool of memory,
no saving left to do.
Deep waters, and I breathe just fine,
but how long before I cross my own line.

And maybe rumors linger true,
I'm caught in rumination,
tethered still to you.

pages.title: Petals
pages.body:
Dazzled by the redness of petals,
An emptiness I couldn't settle.
So I drift where tall grass bends,
Far from voices that let me pretend.

Depth in shadows, sorrow still,
I meet my gaze against my will.
Scared by the redness of petals,
I stay away from flowers,
Hoping to get lost in dusty showers.

Adrift from my own identity,
afraid of light and its shine —
how do I give meaning to this life?

Sand that I touch,
flooded with memories,
a mental loop keeps me stuck.

Flowers die,
leaving without notice —
no answer to my whys.

pages.title: Serious Delusion
pages.body:
Serious delusion,
I talk to plants,
Like I am still holding you in my hands.
Repeat your name in empty rooms,
Watch it fade like lovely afternoons.

I talk to my cat,
Outrunning my own life,
She is scared to see me playing with knives.
But what good comes out of this red.
I don't regret what I said —
Yet why am I addicted to see my skin turn blue,
Sedative state, all I see is you.

Serious delusion,
Hoping for clarity.
Dying to get rid of all confusion,
I am losing my sanity!
Rumination of you on repeat,
Every thought leads to grief.
Craving for the familiarity,
Maybe I am losing gravity.

Serious delusion,
I talk in my sleep,
No, I don't shed tears,
Yet I weep,
I see red and blue all over,
It did happen before,
Got out of it cause I wasn't sure,
Now, it has happened again —
Gotten out of control
And it feels like a war,
Whom do I kill,
What pill do I take,
For goodness sake.

Serious delusion,
I talk to you in my sleep,
Listening to your voice on repeat.

Serious delusion,
Running out of tears,
Nothing left to weep.

pages.title: Embedded
pages.body:
You are embedded in my heart and brain,
No matter how I try, it ends in vain.
I cannot bring myself to feel disdain,
These pulling feelings drag me back again.

I hide a few, pretend I'm still okay,
But something in me always slips your way.
Who am I beneath this strange display?
What brought me here, what was I meant to say?

With every breath, a memory escapes,
Yet countless more refuse to lose their shapes.
This emptiness is more than what it seems,
It echoes you in fragments and in dreams.

It's not just sorrow filling up my chest,
It's you returning when I need you least.
And still I wait, as if you would return without obligation,
Veins running out of blood, life losing its patience.

pages.title: A Curse of Writing
pages.body:
A curse of writing you,
I find you in every word.
A rhythm of feelings —
the more I write,
the more I delay healing.

Every verse I write
somehow returns to you;
it says I miss you,
but I don't wish that you do.

A craving that eats me hollow,
like a wolf under a full moon —
I howl.

I prefer penance over acceptance,
for when it's about you,
I can never finish the sentence.

Who knows if there'll be a period —
or if this just goes on,
words: some serious and some inferior.

pages.title: Waterfalls
pages.body:
Down waterfalls, the stone is worn away,
Each drop inscribes what time cannot delay;
The leaves, instead, embrace the silver spray,
And hold each trembling gift that comes their way.

Yet in the storm, their nature turns to fight,
They rise as one, accusing with their might;
No trace of calm, no memory of light,
But sharpened words that pierce the endless night.

The sky looks on and pities what they grow,
A quiet grief for all it comes to know;
It watches change unfold in silent flow,
As kindness fades beneath resentment's glow.

The witness drowns, yet still recalls the rain,
It speaks in drops that echo loss and pain;
Though all may turn and cast their faults again,
The rain remembers, time repeats the stain.

pages.title: These Flowers
pages.body:
These flowers don't speak to me,
The way they used to,
At my every glimpse,
They start to wither,
As if I am autumn,
And they ought to die in drought.

These flowers don't speak to me the way they used to,
At every glance, petals wither away,
And the remaining are very few,
I think I should accept their death,
But leaving is something I am not used to.

pages.title: Within Madness
pages.body:
Within madness, I stand with stillness,
With water in one and sand in another hand.
All slipping away, lost as my motherland.
Deemed to be something, my room is filled with darkness.

In a corner, you will find me with nothing and everything at once.
All in, I had lost all my chips in poker.
Sanity within me has lost its cause, as if it has spiritually failed.
I sit with my thoughts in temples,
While my hand trembles.
For if there is a God, He must question what I gambled.

At 2:00 a.m., find me wondering about tomorrow.
A past with hauntingly beautiful memories drowning in today's pain,
A feeling of standing in pouring rain —
I hover around my own heart,
So I ask myself:
How do I put it in simple words what hurts?

pages.title: Until When
pages.body:
Until when will you run,
Aren't you tired
Of all the materialist things?
All the party people
You have surrounded yourself by —
Do they give you space?
I know I said life isn't a race
But you can't just run forever.

On the shore, looking at the sunset,
I am standing in rain,
My skin is wet.
Who knows if tears would make me fall for you
Or make me fall apart.

Until when will you run,
From things that just settle down in heart forever,
You were clever, what happened to you,
Where did you run to?

Come back,
My arms are tired.
I don't hope for you to come back
But I hope you don't get lost with the wind of today.
With a dying melody, which is hollow,
I leave you to tomorrow.

pages.title: Fragile
pages.body:
Words, so fragile and broken,
Actions, so stoic and hollow.
Between them, the lines blur,
Who knows what I feel?
Who knows what I did.

In an abyss of words,
I am astray, I have lost my way.
Who knows where I am,
Who knows where home is,
and how far.

Every dusk, drowning in words,
Looking for something to hold,
But which shore am I looking for?
The one where I will turn into ash,
Or the one where I can lose my memories in a flash.

Who knows, where is my mind,
Who knows, where is my heart,
Who knows, where is my soul.

pages.title: Masquerade
pages.body:
Not obvious —
it lives hauntingly inside,
a quiet plague beneath the skin.
All the party people
can't see behind the mask,
neither can I.

A walk down a downtown aisle,
faces lit, watching —
my expression falters.
All the party people
wither as the music slows,
and I...
I stand with a melody echoing in my head,
a chanting,
a chorus,
a prayer.

Every beat feels like a needle in the heart,
yet safer than letting the guard fall apart.
My masquerade doesn't drop;
it has merged,
a refuge from all emotion.
Once inseparable devotion,
now in vain, every breath
I take with caution.

pages.title: Walls
pages.body:
These walls are better than humans,
or so I say.
Humans are afraid,
these walls are not.

They see me weep,
and keep no distance.
Emotions they lack,
yet they have my back.

They see my rage,
they see my will to die,
and yet they do not cry.
They watch my sorrow,
carry echoes of hope I borrowed.

They don't grow tired of me,
they are not like humans, you see.
They never turn away,
never close the door on me.

Tell me, are walls kinder than people,
or am I just safer unseen?

pages.title: Crimes
pages.body:
Wither away in time,
for I am weary of my crimes.
Crimes committed in honesty,
with modesty
and the price I paid.
A simple life was all I craved.

In no time, I found myself at the altar,
pleading for shelter.
Too late, the priest told me:
we do not take in prisoners of time.

And I withered into the wind,
for I was too tired
to commit another crime.

pages.title: Gratitude
pages.body:
You show gratitude,
What do you know about love,
How I was killed by my own thoughts
in multitudes,
How I convinced myself it's not loneliness but solitude.

Loved you with what I had,
You said this is not worthy, it's all bad,
And that made me sad,
Loved you with all I had.

On empty roads, I stood still,
Did you see my will?
How I stayed all the time,
While you were committing crimes,
And I was alone and making rhymes.

How I craved for death,
How I craved for love,
How I loved your hate,
In the aftermath of what happened
I still stayed.

Somewhere in my heart I still love you,
And I can tell you I have only loved a few.
Still hope that you will come back running,
And people call me “you are funny” —
I still think there was love and you did love me,
How delusional am I, you see?

Your appreciation killed me,
How I was dying did you realise?
I loved you and you saw that in my eyes,
Still mistaking my loneliness
for solitude.
With hope, ache and love
I am still living in multitude.

What do I do of your gratitude,
The way you didn't fight to stay,
The way you left, and I craved.
What do you know about my love,
What do you know about staying when it hurts.
Take a shovel and dig my grave,
I am done being brave.

pages.title: If Art Survives
pages.body:
Reckless shield of steel,
A masked self behind the wheel,
A silent urge to lose control,
A darker wish that takes its toll.

In fractured thought and numbing still,
A mind that bends against its will,
Where body slows and senses part,
And questions rise of art and heart.

Who remains when all is still?
The voice, the void, the fragile will —
For who can tell, when all has ceased,
If art survives... or kills the artist.

pages.title: The Bridge
pages.body:
I stood at crossroads, staring across a destiny I could not find,
a bridge, my only way to the other side.

I burned it and swallowed the ashes.

Now I burn with what remains:
everything I lost,
and everything I dreamed of.

pages.title: Art and the Artist
pages.body:
On prima facie or otherwise, can you really differentiate between the art and the artist? Not really. The origin of art? That is true and you know what I am about to say.

One may look sideways when it comes to the artist, but can we really ignore the art? Well, if so, then ignorance must be a bliss. An artist for all the right reasons carries deep sorrow somewhere around his art, for that must be an origin to his rose tinted yet melancholic dimensions in which he lives. As for me, these all are just mere words, the true worth is layered somewhere between the spaces I am using in between the lines.

There are ample thoughts that one carries, to illustrate it; that is art. Art in all its forms is just sadness wrapped around small blissful moments where one must carry on, and the artist lives — but the art? Art is immortal. One might be able to kill the artist that lives inside but can he erase the core aspects of his own being? If you ask me, maybe that is not what I reckon, but I guess it is better to kill the artist than the art. The paradox of this chapter lies in the rhetorical questions and the assumptions. As a different point of view, there are endless opportunities to answer these self-originated questions, however, all the attempts will go in vain, as the true one is hidden between the sane and insane.

For I hide underneath my words, define my worth by what I do and what I don't. Even an artist would agree that words without actions are nothing but a mere attempt to consolidate the being of one another. Actions, on the other hand, live in memories, in time, in longing and in sorrow. However, with that being said, words are powerful — but how? When they are defined by actions. When someone says I love you and does so every day. Artists are no different; they love their art and they do so by creating it.

A small story: once a gardener and later a theatre artist — a man lived in a garden full of flowers. They all used to bloom at midnight, but there was no specific time on days where he was not around. Seldom he used to witness it and on others he missed by a minute. He was an actor who had nowhere to go, no one to turn to, no one to love. But because he had to keep living, he had to eat, and to eat he had to work. Working all day, he used to come back right after midnight and go to his favourite bench with a gut full of food but a heart full of regret that he was unable to witness the bloom.

On a random Friday, he woke up by the street close to the theatre he used to work. He decided to give up food. He worked but he was famished, yet he went to the garden. Little did he know, it rained. He did not sleep on an empty stomach — he slept in the rain with an empty stomach. He did so for several days until the monsoon got over, and one day with a clear sky he saw his favourite purple flower bloom, and that was enough for him. He died a few days after, as he had grown addicted to the blooming and forgot that food was important. What he failed to do was recognise the difference between need and want.

So — that is what an artist and art is. Art is an addiction, a desire, a want for the artist. And as for artists? They are the need of the world — a constant reminder of desire, passionate individuals who live by their art and die by their art. Separation between need and want is important for every human being, be it an artist or otherwise, because you can be in love and live for yourself, but you cannot be in love and offer your entire being to the other. Art needs soul. Art needs desire. And most of all, art needs the artist. An artist drowned in art is passion wrapped in obsession. An artist swimming in his art is a man who drowns art in his passion.

Wind has taught me to carry people,
and you taught me how to keep them alive.

Art or the artist?
Artist and the art.
